items.title: Denied Justice — Section Cover
items.desc: Typography-only cover for the "Denied Justice" special section. "When Rape Is Reported And Nothing Happens." Each word on its own line, thin red underline. No image — the words are the image. Deliberate restraint for a subject that demands it. Pulitzer Prize finalist, SPJ Public Service Award, four Minnesota legislative changes.
items.impact: Four Minnesota laws changed. Process became the template for the 2021 Floyd Pulitzer.

items.title: Denied Justice — Sunday A1
items.desc: Front page of the Star Tribune, Sunday July 22, 2018. "The Rape Cases That Go Nowhere." Dominant group portrait by Renee Jones Schneider — survivors photographed together, not as anonymous victims. The section ran inside. The front page made it unavoidable.
items.impact: Front-page placement forced public reckoning with systemic failure in rape investigations.

items.title: Denied Justice — Survivor Portraits
items.desc: Four-portrait grid from the special section. Each woman photographed in her environment — named, with age, profession, and one quote. Katie Finch: "I felt like I wasn't important." Melissa Miyashiro: "The system is broken." The editorial choice: every story gets equal weight.
items.impact: Portraits gave survivors dignity the system had denied them.

items.title: Jihad in the Heartland
items.desc: Investigative series visual package. Illustration direction — the silhouette carries the narrative before the reader reaches the text. Multi-part series design.

items.title: 61 Seconds
items.desc: News illustration direction. Visualizing the 61-second gap between the 911 call and the ambulance dispatch. Data-driven visual storytelling.

items.title: Bees at the Brink
items.desc: Investigative series on colony collapse. Illustration-driven data journalism — the visual package made complex environmental science accessible. OJA Award for Explanatory Reporting.

items.title: A Matter of Dignity
items.desc: Five-part investigation into how Minnesota fails the disabled. Visual direction for the series that became a Pulitzer Prize finalist. Governor responded within one week. Derek credited by name on the OJA entry.
items.impact: Governor responded within one week of publication.

items.title: Sugar Investigation
items.desc: Investigative illustration direction. Conceptual art for an enterprise story — visual metaphor carrying the investigation before the reader reaches the text.

items.title: Prince — April 21, 2016
items.desc: Breaking news visual direction. Full-page illustration commissioned and published within hours of Prince's death. Directed art, typography, and page architecture under deadline.
items.impact: Reprints still selling 10 years later. Became proof of concept for Star Tribune Magazine.

items.title: Prince — Four Zoned Covers
items.desc: Commemorative section with four zoned covers for different regions. Two illustrated by Robert Carter, commissioned under breaking news deadline. Prince died Thursday — this ran Sunday. The editorial choice: illustration over photography. Every other paper ran a wire photo. Art direction under grief.
items.impact: 16-page commemorative section across four regional zones. Reprints still sell today.

items.title: Trump Wave — Election Night
items.desc: Breaking news cover design. Election night art direction — the red wave rendered as visual force. Produced under deadline pressure when the result defied projections.

items.title: Obama Inauguration
items.desc: LA Times special section. Art direction for the inauguration of the first Black president. Visual storytelling for a historic moment — one of several breaking news packages directed during the number-one worldwide era.

items.title: John Wooden — 1910-2010
items.desc: LA Times commemorative cover. The presses were stopped for three hours and fifteen minutes. 175,849 papers went without; 400,416 received the obituary package. Breaking news art direction — the decision to stop the presses and redesign on deadline.
items.impact: Presses stopped 3 hours 15 minutes. 400,416 papers received the redesigned obituary package.

items.title: California Wildfires
items.desc: LA Times double-truck. Photography direction and page architecture for wildfire disaster coverage. Breaking news visual direction at scale.

items.title: The Oscars
items.desc: LA Times poster design for Academy Awards coverage. Part of a visual identity system for event coverage — the same instinct applied to Olympics, elections, and cultural events.

items.title: Sochi Olympics — The Flame
items.desc: Olympics coverage package. Mosaic illustration — the Olympic flame built from Russian cultural icons. Directed cross-platform visual strategy for 17-day event.

items.title: Olympics — Diving
items.desc: Olympics coverage poster series. Photo editing and layout — the yellow board, the blue water, the frozen moment before departure.

items.title: Olympics — USA Basketball
items.desc: Olympics coverage poster series. Photo editing and typography. Part of a cross-platform visual identity system for Games coverage.

items.title: Star Tribune Magazine
items.desc: Self-initiated editorial product. Built from prototype to Sunday anchor — a new franchise for 225,000+ subscribers. Debut cover: Prince, one year after the commemorative section proved the concept. Program-building, not just design.
items.impact: New franchise product for 225,000+ Sunday subscribers.

items.title: Minnesota's Gift to the World
items.desc: Star Tribune Magazine cover. Aerial photography direction for Voyageurs National Park feature. The magazine became the Sunday anchor product under Derek's creative direction.

items.title: Magazine Prototype
items.desc: Magazine concept and design direction. The prototype that became the Star Tribune's Sunday magazine — a new revenue and audience product built from scratch.
items.impact: Prototype greenlit into permanent Sunday franchise.

items.title: 50 Years of Taste
items.desc: Section anniversary visual package. Creative direction for the Taste section's 50th anniversary — editorial and event design.

items.title: Election Data — The Shift
items.desc: Data visualization design. Election results rendered as directional arrows — red right, blue left. Visual system designed for print and digital, showing county-level movement at a glance.

items.title: Weather Data Visualization
items.desc: Information design for daily weather coverage. Data rendered as visual system — readable at a glance, detailed on inspection.

items.title: VR Flyover — U.S. Bank Stadium
items.desc: Emerging technology. Directed one of the first VR experiences in U.S. newspapers. Virtual flyover of the new stadium for desktop and mobile.
items.impact: One of the first VR experiences in U.S. newspapers.

items.title: There's No Mistaking Love
items.desc: Los Angeles Times feature illustration. Commissioned and directed. The newspaper as landscape — two figures navigating the page itself. During the six consecutive years the LA Times ranked #1 worldwide in design (SND).

items.title: Showtime — Art Direction
items.desc: Cover illustration art direction. Commissioned and directed for the LA Times during the number-one worldwide era. Working with illustrators to translate editorial vision into published art.

items.title: Small Business Inc.
items.desc: Business section illustration direction. Conceptual art for an enterprise story on small business survival — the castle under siege.

items.title: State Fair — Congregate Then Hibernate
items.desc: Product design and brand extension. Minnesota State Fair merchandise program — $2M+ annual revenue. Journalism funded by design.
items.impact: $2M+ annual revenue. Journalism funded by design.

items.title: claudewill.io
items.desc: AI platform built from scratch. Custom CMS, conversation engine (CW's Porch), 11 published essays, overnight agent pipeline, kitchen dashboard. No framework — vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, Netlify Functions, Anthropic API. The methodology is the product.

items.title: Cascadia Daily News
items.desc: Client consulting work. Cover illustration and visual strategy for Cascadia Daily News. Active engagement through CW Strategies.

items.title: The CW Standard
items.desc: Brand identity design. The five-principle ethical framework — designed for digital, print, and social distribution.

items.title: Coach D
items.desc: Basketball coaching. The second career.

items.title: First Byline
items.desc: Age 18. The Hutchinson News. Where it started — part-time sports writer while at community college.

items.title: Panthers Basketball
items.desc: Hutchinson Community College. Before the newsroom.

items.title: Little Star
items.desc: Before any of it.

items.title: The Builder
items.desc: Thinking about something. Probably how to take it apart.

items.title: Father and Son
items.desc: Under the banyan tree.

items.title: The Dog
items.desc: Dog lover. Raptors hat. Grass.

items.title: Driveway
items.desc: Where the coaching started.

items.title: The Shirt
items.desc: David with bubblegum.
